`services/analytics.service.ts`:

```ts
   1 | // db/services/analytics.service.ts
   2 | // Pure business logic for analytics - no direct DB calls
   3 | 
   4 | import type { AnalyticsRepo } from "../queries/analytics.repo";
   5 | 
   6 | export interface TrendData {
   7 | 	date: Date;
   8 | 	value: number;
   9 | 	label: string;
  10 | }
  11 | 
  12 | export interface PredictionData {
  13 | 	date: Date;
  14 | 	predictedValue: number;
  15 | 	lowerBound: number;
  16 | 	upperBound: number;
  17 | 	confidence: number;
  18 | }
  19 | 
  20 | export interface DateRange {
  21 | 	start: Date;
  22 | 	end: Date;
  23 | 	label: string;
  24 | }
  25 | 
  26 | export interface GrowthMetrics {
  27 | 	patientsTracked: number;
  28 | 	below5thPercentile: number;
  29 | 	above95thPercentile: number;
  30 | 	growthVelocityNormal: number;
  31 | 	concernsIdentified: number;
  32 | }
  33 | 
  34 | export interface WellnessMetrics {
  35 | 	completedVisits: number;
  36 | 	recommendedVisits: number;
  37 | 	complianceRate: number;
  38 | 	earlyDetectionRate: number;
  39 | }
  40 | 
  41 | export interface TreatmentMetrics {
  42 | 	prescriptionAdherence: number;
  43 | 	hospitalizationRate: number;
  44 | 	erVisitsRate: number;
  45 | 	followUpCompliance: number;
  46 | }
  47 | 
  48 | export interface OverallMetrics {
  49 | 	patientSatisfaction: number;
  50 | 	careQualityScore: number;
  51 | 	outcomeImprovement: number;
  52 | }
  53 | 
  54 | /**
  55 |  * Service: Analytics Business Logic
  56 |  * - No direct DB calls
  57 |  * - Pure calculations, transformations, and predictions
  58 |  */
  59 | export class AnalyticsService {
  60 | 	constructor(private readonly analyticsRepo: AnalyticsRepo) {}
  61 | 
  62 | 	/**
  63 | 	 * Calculate date range based on period
  64 | 	 */
  65 | 	calculateDateRange(period: string, startDate?: Date, endDate?: Date): DateRange {
  66 | 		const end = endDate || new Date();
  67 | 		const start =
  68 | 			startDate ||
  69 | 			(() => {
  70 | 				const d = new Date();
  71 | 				switch (period) {
  72 | 					case "week":
  73 | 						d.setDate(d.getDate() - 7);
  74 | 						break;
  75 | 					case "month":
  76 | 						d.setMonth(d.getMonth() - 1);
  77 | 						break;
  78 | 					case "quarter":
  79 | 						d.setMonth(d.getMonth() - 3);
  80 | 						break;
  81 | 					case "year":
  82 | 						d.setFullYear(d.getFullYear() - 1);
  83 | 						break;
  84 | 					case "6months":
  85 | 						d.setMonth(d.getMonth() - 6);
  86 | 						break;
  87 | 					default:
  88 | 						d.setMonth(d.getMonth() - 1);
  89 | 				}
  90 | 				return d;
  91 | 			})();
  92 | 
  93 | 		return {
  94 | 			start,
  95 | 			end,
  96 | 			label: `${start.toLocaleDateString()} - ${end.toLocaleDateString()}`
  97 | 		};
  98 | 	}
  99 | 
 100 | 	/**
 101 | 	 * Get previous period start date
 102 | 	 */
 103 | 	getPreviousPeriodStart(start: Date, end: Date): Date {
 104 | 		const duration = end.getTime() - start.getTime();
 105 | 		return new Date(start.getTime() - duration);
 106 | 	}
 107 | 
 108 | 	/**
 109 | 	 * Group data by date (day, week, or month)
 110 | 	 */
 111 | 	groupDataByDate<T extends { date?: Date; appointmentDate?: Date; createdAt?: Date }>(
 112 | 		items: T[],
 113 | 		groupBy: string,
 114 | 		valueExtractor: (item: T) => number
 115 | 	): TrendData[] {
 116 | 		const grouped = new Map<string, number>();
 117 | 
 118 | 		for (const item of items) {
 119 | 			let key: string;
 120 | 			const rawDate = item.date || item.appointmentDate || item.createdAt;
 121 | 			if (!rawDate) continue;
 122 | 
 123 | 			const date = new Date(rawDate);
 124 | 
 125 | 			if (groupBy === "day") {
 126 | 				key = date.toISOString().split("T")[0] || "";
 127 | 			} else if (groupBy === "week") {
 128 | 				const weekStart = new Date(date);
 129 | 				weekStart.setDate(date.getDate() - date.getDay());
 130 | 				key = weekStart.toISOString().split("T")[0] || "";
 131 | 			} else {
 132 | 				key = `${date.getFullYear()}-${date.getMonth() + 1}`;
 133 | 			}
 134 | 
 135 | 			grouped.set(key, (grouped.get(key) || 0) + valueExtractor(item));
 136 | 		}
 137 | 
 138 | 		return Array.from(grouped.entries())
 139 | 			.map(([key, value]) => ({
 140 | 				date: new Date(key),
 141 | 				value,
 142 | 				label: key
 143 | 			}))
 144 | 			.sort((a, b) => a.date.getTime() - b.date.getTime());
 145 | 	}
 146 | 
 147 | 	/**
 148 | 	 * Calculate age in months from date of birth
 149 | 	 */
 150 | 	calculateAgeInMonths(dateOfBirth: Date, asOfDate: Date = new Date()): number {
 151 | 		const years = asOfDate.getFullYear() - dateOfBirth.getFullYear();
 152 | 		const months = asOfDate.getMonth() - dateOfBirth.getMonth();
 153 | 		return years * 12 + months;
 154 | 	}
 155 | 
 156 | 	/**
 157 | 	 * Calculate age in years from date of birth
 158 | 	 */
 159 | 	calculateAgeInYears(dateOfBirth: Date, asOfDate: Date = new Date()): number {
 160 | 		return this.calculateAgeInMonths(dateOfBirth, asOfDate) / 12;
 161 | 	}
 162 | 
 163 | 	/**
 164 | 	 * Generate predictions from historical data using simple linear projection
 165 | 	 */
 166 | 	generatePredictions(historicalData: TrendData[], horizon: string): PredictionData[] {
 167 | 		if (historicalData.length < 3) return [];
 168 | 
 169 | 		const values = historicalData.map(d => d.value);
 170 | 		const average = values.reduce((a, b) => a + b, 0) / values.length;
 171 | 		const trend = ((values[values.length - 1] ?? 0) - (values[0] ?? 0)) / values.length;
 172 | 
 173 | 		const months = horizon === "week" ? 1 : horizon === "month" ? 3 : 6;
 174 | 		const predictions: PredictionData[] = [];
 175 | 
 176 | 		for (let i = 1; i <= months; i++) {
 177 | 			const predictedValue = average + trend * (values.length + i);
 178 | 			predictions.push({
 179 | 				date: new Date(Date.now() + i * 30 * 24 * 60 * 60 * 1000),
 180 | 				predictedValue: Math.max(0, Math.round(predictedValue)),
 181 | 				lowerBound: Math.max(0, Math.round(predictedValue * 0.8)),
 182 | 				upperBound: Math.round(predictedValue * 1.2),
 183 | 				confidence: Math.max(0.5, 1 - i / (months * 2))
 184 | 			});
 185 | 		}
 186 | 
 187 | 		return predictions;
 188 | 	}
 189 | 
 190 | 	/**
 191 | 	 * Generate insights from predictions
 192 | 	 */
 193 | 	generateInsights(predictions: PredictionData[], metric: string): string[] {
 194 | 		const insights: string[] = [];
 195 | 
 196 | 		if (predictions.length > 0) {
 197 | 			const trend =
 198 | 				(predictions[predictions.length - 1]?.predictedValue ?? 0) - (predictions[0]?.predictedValue ?? 0);
 199 | 			if (trend > 0) {
 200 | 				insights.push(
 201 | 					`📈 Expected ${trend > 10 ? "significant" : "moderate"} growth in ${metric} over the next period`
 202 | 				);
 203 | 			} else if (trend < 0) {
 204 | 				insights.push(
 205 | 					`📉 ${metric} may decrease by ${Math.abs(trend).toFixed(1)}% - consider proactive measures`
 206 | 				);
 207 | 			} else {
 208 | 				insights.push(`📊 ${metric} expected to remain stable`);
 209 | 			}
 210 | 		}
 211 | 
 212 | 		insights.push(`🤖 AI analysis based on ${predictions.length * 2}+ data points`);
 213 | 		return insights;
 214 | 	}
 215 | 
 216 | 	/**
 217 | 	 * Generate recommendations based on metric and predictions
 218 | 	 */
 219 | 	generateRecommendations(predictions: PredictionData[], metric: string): string[] {
 220 | 		const recommendations: string[] = [];
 221 | 
 222 | 		const hasGrowth =
 223 | 			predictions.length > 0 &&
 224 | 			(predictions[predictions.length - 1]?.predictedValue ?? 0) > (predictions[0]?.predictedValue ?? 0);
 225 | 
 226 | 		if (metric === "appointments") {
 227 | 			if (hasGrowth) {
 228 | 				recommendations.push("Consider adjusting staffing levels during predicted peak periods");
 229 | 			}
 230 | 			recommendations.push("Implement automated reminder system to reduce no-shows");
 231 | 		} else if (metric === "patients") {
 232 | 			if (hasGrowth) {
 233 | 				recommendations.push("Expand marketing efforts in predicted growth areas");
 234 | 			}
 235 | 			recommendations.push("Ensure sufficient provider capacity for expected patient influx");
 236 | 		} else if (metric === "revenue") {
 237 | 			if (hasGrowth) {
 238 | 				recommendations.push("Optimize billing processes for predicted transaction volume");
 239 | 			}
 240 | 			recommendations.push("Consider new service lines based on demand projections");
 241 | 		}
 242 | 
 243 | 		return recommendations;
 244 | 	}
 245 | 
 246 | 	/**
 247 | 	 * Calculate expected growth percentage
 248 | 	 */
 249 | 	calculateExpectedGrowth(historicalData: TrendData[], predictions: PredictionData[]): number {
 250 | 		if (historicalData.length === 0 || predictions.length === 0) return 0;
 251 | 		const lastHistorical = historicalData[historicalData.length - 1]?.value ?? 0;
 252 | 		const lastPredicted = predictions[predictions.length - 1]?.predictedValue ?? 0;
 253 | 		return lastHistorical > 0 ? ((lastPredicted - lastHistorical) / lastHistorical) * 100 : 0;
 254 | 	}
 255 | 
 256 | 	/**
 257 | 	 * Calculate confidence level based on historical data points
 258 | 	 */
 259 | 	calculateConfidenceLevel(historicalData: TrendData[]): number {
 260 | 		if (historicalData.length < 6) return 0.65;
 261 | 		if (historicalData.length < 12) return 0.75;
 262 | 		return 0.85;
 263 | 	}
 264 | 
 265 | 	/**
 266 | 	 * Find peak period from predictions
 267 | 	 */
 268 | 	findPeakPeriod(predictions: PredictionData[]): string | undefined {
 269 | 		const first = predictions[0];
 270 | 		if (!first) return undefined;
 271 | 		const maxPrediction = predictions.reduce((max, p) => (p.predictedValue > max.predictedValue ? p : max), first);
 272 | 		return maxPrediction.date.toLocaleString("default", { month: "long" });
 273 | 	}
 274 | 
 275 | 	/**
 276 | 	 * Identify risk factors from predictions
 277 | 	 */
 278 | 	identifyRiskFactors(predictions: PredictionData[], metric: string): string[] {
 279 | 		const risks = [];
 280 | 
 281 | 		if (metric === "appointments" && predictions.length > 0) {
 282 | 			const maxPrediction = Math.max(...predictions.map(p => p.predictedValue));
 283 | 			const minPrediction = Math.min(...predictions.map(p => p.predictedValue));
 284 | 			if (maxPrediction - minPrediction > 50) {
 285 | 				risks.push("High variability in appointment demand");
 286 | 			}
 287 | 		}
 288 | 
 289 | 		return risks;
 290 | 	}
 291 | 
 292 | 	/**
 293 | 	 * Calculate patient risk score based on medical history
 294 | 	 */
 295 | 	calculatePatientRiskScore(patientData: {
 296 | 		medicalRecords?: unknown[];
 297 | 		prescriptions?: { status: string }[];
 298 | 		allergies?: unknown[];
 299 | 	}): number {
 300 | 		let riskScore = 50;
 301 | 
 302 | 		if ((patientData.medicalRecords?.length ?? 0) > 5) riskScore += 10;
 303 | 		if (patientData.prescriptions?.some(p => p.status === "active")) riskScore += 5;
 304 | 		if ((patientData.allergies?.length ?? 0) > 0) riskScore += 15;
 305 | 
 306 | 		return Math.min(100, riskScore);
 307 | 	}
 308 | 
 309 | 	/**
 310 | 	 * Generate patient outcome predictions
 311 | 	 */
 312 | 	generatePatientOutcomes(
 313 | 		_patientData: unknown,
 314 | 		_condition?: string,
 315 | 		horizon?: string
 316 | 	): Array<{
 317 | 		outcome: string;
 318 | 		probability: number;
 319 | 		timeframe: string;
 320 | 		severity: string;
 321 | 	}> {
 322 | 		return [
 323 | 			{
 324 | 				outcome: "Improved condition",
 325 | 				probability: 0.65,
 326 | 				timeframe: horizon || "month",
 327 | 				severity: "low"
 328 | 			},
 329 | 			{
 330 | 				outcome: "Stable condition",
 331 | 				probability: 0.25,
 332 | 				timeframe: horizon || "month",
 333 | 				severity: "low"
 334 | 			},
 335 | 			{
 336 | 				outcome: "Requires intervention",
 337 | 				probability: 0.1,
 338 | 				timeframe: horizon || "month",
 339 | 				severity: "medium"
 340 | 			}
 341 | 		];
 342 | 	}
 343 | 
 344 | 	/**
 345 | 	 * Generate patient recommendations based on risk score
 346 | 	 */
 347 | 	generatePatientRecommendations(_patientData: unknown, riskScore: number, _outcomes: unknown[]): string[] {
 348 | 		const recommendations = [];
 349 | 
 350 | 		if (riskScore > 70) {
 351 | 			recommendations.push("Schedule follow-up within 2 weeks for close monitoring");
 352 | 			recommendations.push("Consider specialist consultation for high-risk factors");
 353 | 		} else if (riskScore > 50) {
 354 | 			recommendations.push("Regular monthly monitoring recommended");
 355 | 			recommendations.push("Review treatment plan at next visit");
 356 | 		} else {
 357 | 			recommendations.push("Continue current management plan");
 358 | 			recommendations.push("Annual wellness visit recommended");
 359 | 		}
 360 | 
 361 | 		return recommendations;
 362 | 	}
 363 | 
 364 | 	/**
 365 | 	 * Create monitoring schedule based on risk score
 366 | 	 */
 367 | 	createMonitoringSchedule(
 368 | 		patientData: { growthRecords?: unknown[] },
 369 | 		riskScore: number
 370 | 	): Array<{
 371 | 		metric: string;
 372 | 		frequency: string;
 373 | 		target: string;
 374 | 	}> {
 375 | 		const schedule = [
 376 | 			{
 377 | 				metric: "Vital signs",
 378 | 				frequency: riskScore > 70 ? "Weekly" : "Monthly",
 379 | 				target: "Normal range"
 380 | 			},
 381 | 			{ metric: "Medication adherence", frequency: "Monthly", target: ">80%" }
 382 | 		];
 383 | 
 384 | 		if ((patientData.growthRecords?.length ?? 0) > 0) {
 385 | 			schedule.push({
 386 | 				metric: "Growth parameters",
 387 | 				frequency: "3 months",
 388 | 				target: "Following percentile"
 389 | 			});
 390 | 		}
 391 | 
 392 | 		return schedule;
 393 | 	}
 394 | 
 395 | 	/**
 396 | 	 * Calculate age distribution from patient data
 397 | 	 */
 398 | 	calculateAgeDistribution(
 399 | 		patients: Array<{ dateOfBirth: Date; createdAt: Date }>,
 400 | 		ageGroups: Array<{ group: string; minMonths: number; maxMonths: number }>
 401 | 	): Array<{ group: string; count: number; percentage: number }> {
 402 | 		const distribution = ageGroups.map(group => ({
 403 | 			group: group.group,
 404 | 			count: 0,
 405 | 			percentage: 0
 406 | 		}));
 407 | 
 408 | 		for (const patient of patients) {
 409 | 			const ageMonths = this.calculateAgeInMonths(patient.dateOfBirth, patient.createdAt);
 410 | 			const group = ageGroups.find(g => ageMonths >= g.minMonths && ageMonths < g.maxMonths);
 411 | 			if (group) {
 412 | 				const idx = distribution.findIndex(d => d.group === group.group);
 413 | 				if (idx !== -1) distribution[idx]?.count ?? 0;
 414 | 			}
 415 | 		}
 416 | 
 417 | 		const total = distribution.reduce((sum, d) => sum + d.count, 0);
 418 | 		distribution.forEach(d => {
 419 | 			d.percentage = total > 0 ? (d.count / total) * 100 : 0;
 420 | 		});
 421 | 
 422 | 		return distribution;
 423 | 	}
 424 | 
 425 | 	/**
 426 | 	 * Calculate trend direction based on current and previous values
 427 | 	 */
 428 | 	calculateTrend(current: number, previous: number): "improving" | "declining" | "stable" {
 429 | 		if (current > previous * 1.05) return "improving";
 430 | 		if (current < previous * 0.95) return "declining";
 431 | 		return "stable";
 432 | 	}
 433 | 
 434 | 	/**
 435 | 	 * Generate benchmark recommendations
 436 | 	 */
 437 | 	generateBenchmarkRecommendations(metric: string, status: string, trend: string): string[] {
 438 | 		const recommendations = [];
 439 | 
 440 | 		if (status === "below") {
 441 | 			recommendations.push(`Develop action plan to improve ${metric} performance`);
 442 | 			recommendations.push("Analyze best practices from top-performing clinics");
 443 | 		} else if (status === "above" && trend === "improving") {
 444 | 			recommendations.push("Continue current strategies and document successes");
 445 | 			recommendations.push("Share best practices with other departments");
 446 | 		} else if (trend === "declining") {
 447 | 			recommendations.push("Conduct root cause analysis for declining metrics");
 448 | 			recommendations.push("Implement corrective measures immediately");
 449 | 		}
 450 | 
 451 | 		return recommendations;
 452 | 	}
 453 | 
 454 | 	/**
 455 | 	 * Calculate completion rate from stats
 456 | 	 */
 457 | 	calculateCompletionRate(completed: number, total: number): number {
 458 | 		return total > 0 ? Math.round((completed / total) * 100 * 10) / 10 : 0;
 459 | 	}
 460 | 
 461 | 	/**
 462 | 	 * Calculate average revenue per completed appointment
 463 | 	 */
 464 | 	calculateAverageRevenuePerAppointment(totalRevenue: number, completedAppointments: number): number {
 465 | 		return completedAppointments > 0 ? totalRevenue / completedAppointments : 0;
 466 | 	}
 467 | 
 468 | 	async computePatientGrowthTrends(clinicId: string, period: string): Promise<TrendData[]> {
 469 | 		// 1. Orchestrate: Determine the business logic boundaries
 470 | 		const { start, end } = this.calculateDateRange(period);
 471 | 
 472 | 		// 2. Fetch: Delegate data retrieval to the Repository Layer
 473 | 		const patients = await this.analyticsRepo.getPatientsInDateRange(clinicId, start, end);
 474 | 
 475 | 		// 3. Transform: Process raw data into the format needed by the UI
 476 | 		return this.groupDataByDate(patients, "month", () => 1);
 477 | 	}
 478 | }
 479 | 
 480 | // Export singleton instance
 481 | export const analyticsService = new AnalyticsService({} as AnalyticsRepo); // db/services/analytics.service.ts
 482 | // Pure business logic for analytics - no direct DB calls
 483 | 
 484 | export interface TrendData {
 485 | 	date: Date;
 486 | 	value: number;
 487 | 	label: string;
 488 | }
 489 | 
 490 | export interface PredictionData {
 491 | 	date: Date;
 492 | 	predictedValue: number;
 493 | 	lowerBound: number;
 494 | 	upperBound: number;
 495 | 	confidence: number;
 496 | }
 497 | 
 498 | export interface DateRange {
 499 | 	start: Date;
 500 | 	end: Date;
 501 | 	label: string;
 502 | }
 503 | 
 504 | export interface GrowthMetrics {
 505 | 	patientsTracked: number;
 506 | 	below5thPercentile: number;
 507 | 	above95thPercentile: number;
 508 | 	growthVelocityNormal: number;
 509 | 	concernsIdentified: number;
 510 | }
 511 | 
 512 | export interface WellnessMetrics {
 513 | 	completedVisits: number;
 514 | 	recommendedVisits: number;
 515 | 	complianceRate: number;
 516 | 	earlyDetectionRate: number;
 517 | }
 518 | 
 519 | export interface TreatmentMetrics {
 520 | 	prescriptionAdherence: number;
 521 | 	hospitalizationRate: number;
 522 | 	erVisitsRate: number;
 523 | 	followUpCompliance: number;
 524 | }
 525 | 
 526 | export interface OverallMetrics {
 527 | 	patientSatisfaction: number;
 528 | 	careQualityScore: number;
 529 | 	outcomeImprovement: number;
 530 | }

```

`services/appointment.service.ts`:

```ts
   1 | // db/services/appointment.service.ts
   2 | // Pure business logic - database agnostic where possible
   3 | 
   4 | import type { DBorTx } from "../client";
   5 | import type { AppointmentRepo } from "../queries/appointment.repo";
   6 | import type { AppointmentStatus } from "../schema";
   7 | import type { DbAppointment, TimeSlot } from "../schema/types";
   8 | 
   9 | /**
  10 |  * Service: Appointment Business Logic
  11 |  * - No direct DB calls
  12 |  * - No drizzle-orm imports
  13 |  * - Pure calculations and validations
  14 |  */
  15 | export class AppointmentService {
  16 | 	constructor(private readonly appointmentRepo: AppointmentRepo) {}
  17 | 
  18 | 	/**
  19 | 	 * Validate appointment time slot availability
  20 | 	 * Pure validation logic - uses repo for checking conflicts
  21 | 	 */
  22 | 	async validateTimeSlot(
  23 | 		doctorId: string,
  24 | 		appointmentDate: Date,
  25 | 		durationMinutes: number,
  26 | 		clinicId: string,
  27 | 		excludeAppointmentId?: string,
  28 | 		tx?: DBorTx
  29 | 	): Promise<{ available: boolean; conflictReason?: string }> {
  30 | 		const isAvailable = await this.appointmentRepo.checkTimeSlotAvailability(
  31 | 			doctorId,
  32 | 			appointmentDate,
  33 | 			durationMinutes,
  34 | 			clinicId,
  35 | 			excludeAppointmentId,
  36 | 			tx
  37 | 		);
  38 | 
  39 | 		if (!isAvailable) {
  40 | 			return {
  41 | 				available: false,
  42 | 				conflictReason: "Time slot already booked for this doctor"
  43 | 			};
  44 | 		}
  45 | 
  46 | 		return { available: true };
  47 | 	}
  48 | 
  49 | 	/**
  50 | 	 * Calculate appointment end time based on start time and duration
  51 | 	 */
  52 | 	calculateEndTime(startDate: Date, durationMinutes: number): Date {
  53 | 		return new Date(startDate.getTime() + durationMinutes * 60000);
  54 | 	}
  55 | 
  56 | 	/**
  57 | 	 * Validate appointment date is not in the past
  58 | 	 */
  59 | 	validateAppointmentDate(date: Date): { valid: boolean; error?: string } {
  60 | 		const now = new Date();
  61 | 		const today = new Date(now.getFullYear(), now.getMonth(), now.getDate());
  62 | 		const appointmentDay = new Date(date.getFullYear(), date.getMonth(), date.getDate());
  63 | 
  64 | 		if (appointmentDay < today) {
  65 | 			return {
  66 | 				valid: false,
  67 | 				error: "Cannot schedule appointments in the past"
  68 | 			};
  69 | 		}
  70 | 
  71 | 		return { valid: true };
  72 | 	}
  73 | 
  74 | 	/**
  75 | 	 * Generate available time slots based on working hours and existing appointments
  76 | 	 */
  77 | 	generateAvailableSlots(
  78 | 		workingStart: string,
  79 | 		workingEnd: string,
  80 | 		durationMinutes: number,
  81 | 		bookedSlots: TimeSlot[]
  82 | 	): TimeSlot[] {
  83 | 		const slots: TimeSlot[] = [];
  84 | 
  85 | 		// Parse working hours
  86 | 		const [startHour, startMinute] = workingStart.split(":").map(Number);
  87 | 		const [endHour, endMinute] = workingEnd.split(":").map(Number);
  88 | 
  89 | 		const workingStartTime = new Date();
  90 | 		workingStartTime.setHours(startHour ?? 0, startMinute ?? 0, 0, 0);
  91 | 
  92 | 		const workingEndTime = new Date();
  93 | 		workingEndTime.setHours(endHour ?? 0, endMinute ?? 0, 0, 0);
  94 | 
  95 | 		// Sort booked slots by start time
  96 | 		const sortedBooked = [...bookedSlots].sort((a, b) => a.start.getTime() - b.start.getTime());
  97 | 
  98 | 		let currentStart = new Date(workingStartTime);
  99 | 
 100 | 		for (const booked of sortedBooked) {
 101 | 			const bookedEnd = new Date(booked.start.getTime() + booked.end.getTime() - booked.start.getTime());
 102 | 
 103 | 			// Check slot before booked appointment
 104 | 			if (currentStart < booked.start) {
 105 | 				const slotEnd = new Date(currentStart.getTime() + durationMinutes * 60000);
 106 | 				if (slotEnd <= booked.start) {
 107 | 					slots.push({ start: new Date(currentStart), end: slotEnd });
 108 | 				}
 109 | 				currentStart = bookedEnd;
 110 | 			} else if (currentStart < bookedEnd) {
 111 | 				currentStart = bookedEnd;
 112 | 			}
 113 | 		}
 114 | 
 115 | 		// Check remaining time after last booked appointment
 116 | 		while (currentStart.getTime() + durationMinutes * 60000 <= workingEndTime.getTime()) {
 117 | 			const slotEnd = new Date(currentStart.getTime() + durationMinutes * 60000);
 118 | 			slots.push({ start: new Date(currentStart), end: slotEnd });
 119 | 			currentStart = slotEnd;
 120 | 		}
 121 | 
 122 | 		return slots;
 123 | 	}
 124 | 
 125 | 	/**
 126 | 	 * Calculate appointment pricing based on service and doctor fees
 127 | 	 */
 128 | 	calculateAppointmentPrice(doctorPrice?: number | null, servicePrice?: number | null): number {
 129 | 		let total = 0;
 130 | 		if (doctorPrice && doctorPrice > 0) total += doctorPrice;
 131 | 		if (servicePrice && servicePrice > 0) total += servicePrice;
 132 | 		return total;
 133 | 	}
 134 | 
 135 | 	/**
 136 | 	 * Determine appointment status based on date and current time
 137 | 	 */
 138 | 	determineAppointmentStatus(appointmentDate: Date, currentStatus?: AppointmentStatus): AppointmentStatus {
 139 | 		// Don't override cancelled/completed statuses
 140 | 		if (currentStatus === "CANCELLED" || currentStatus === "COMPLETED") {
 141 | 			return currentStatus;
 142 | 		}
 143 | 
 144 | 		const now = new Date();
 145 | 
 146 | 		if (appointmentDate < now) {
 147 | 			return "COMPLETED";
 148 | 		}
 149 | 
 150 | 		return currentStatus ?? "PENDING";
 151 | 	}
 152 | 
 153 | 	/**
 154 | 	 * Calculate days until appointment
 155 | 	 */
 156 | 	getDaysUntilAppointment(appointmentDate: Date): number {
 157 | 		const now = new Date();
 158 | 		const diffTime = appointmentDate.getTime() - now.getTime();
 159 | 		return Math.ceil(diffTime / (1000 * 60 * 60 * 24));
 160 | 	}
 161 | 
 162 | 	/**
 163 | 	 * Check if appointment is due for reminder
 164 | 	 */
 165 | 	isReminderDue(appointmentDate: Date, reminderHours: number[] = [24, 2]): number | null {
 166 | 		const now = new Date();
 167 | 		const hoursUntil = (appointmentDate.getTime() - now.getTime()) / (1000 * 60 * 60);
 168 | 
 169 | 		for (const hours of reminderHours) {
 170 | 			if (hoursUntil <= hours && hoursUntil > hours - 1) {
 171 | 				return hours;
 172 | 			}
 173 | 		}
 174 | 
 175 | 		return null;
 176 | 	}
 177 | 
 178 | 	/**
 179 | 	 * Validate reschedule request
 180 | 	 */
 181 | 	validateReschedule(
 182 | 		currentAppointment: DbAppointment,
 183 | 		newDate: Date,
 184 | 		newTime: string
 185 | 	): { valid: boolean; error?: string } {
 186 | 		const dateValidation = this.validateAppointmentDate(newDate);
 187 | 		if (!dateValidation.valid) {
 188 | 			return dateValidation;
 189 | 		}
 190 | 
 191 | 		// Check if the new date/time is different from current
 192 | 		const currentDateStr = currentAppointment.appointmentDate.toISOString().split("T")[0];
 193 | 		const newDateStr = newDate.toISOString().split("T")[0];
 194 | 
 195 | 		if (currentDateStr === newDateStr && currentAppointment.time === newTime) {
 196 | 			return {
 197 | 				valid: false,
 198 | 				error: "New appointment time is the same as current"
 199 | 			};
 200 | 		}
 201 | 
 202 | 		return { valid: true };
 203 | 	}
 204 | 
 205 | 	/**
 206 | 	 * Generate appointment summary for notifications
 207 | 	 */
 208 | 	generateAppointmentSummary(
 209 | 		appointment: Pick<DbAppointment, "appointmentDate" | "time" | "type" | "id">,
 210 | 		patientName: string,
 211 | 		doctorName: string
 212 | 	): string {
 213 | 		const formattedDate = appointment.appointmentDate.toLocaleDateString();
 214 | 		return `Appointment #${appointment.id.slice(0, 8)} scheduled for ${formattedDate} at ${appointment.time} to ${patientName} with Dr. ${doctorName} (Type: ${appointment.type})`;
 215 | 	}
 216 | }
 217 | 
 218 | // Export singleton instance
 219 | export const appointmentService = new AppointmentService(
 220 | 	{} as AppointmentRepo // This will be properly injected
 221 | );

```

`services/auth.service.ts`:

```ts
   1 | // db/services/auth.service.ts
   2 | // Pure business logic for authentication
   3 | 
   4 | import type { AuthRepo } from "../queries";
   5 | import { adminRepo } from "../queries/admin.repo";
   6 | import type { Role } from "../schema";
   7 | import type { NewDbInvite } from "../schema/types";
   8 | 
   9 | export interface InviteValidationResult {
  10 | 	valid: boolean;
  11 | 	error?: string;
  12 | 	invite?: NewDbInvite;
  13 | }
  14 | 
  15 | export interface UserCreationResult {
  16 | 	userId: string;
  17 | 	clinicId: string;
  18 | 	role: Role;
  19 | }
  20 | 
  21 | /**
  22 |  * Service: Authentication Business Logic
  23 |  * - No direct DB calls
  24 |  * - Pure validations and transformations
  25 |  */
  26 | export class AuthService {
  27 | 	constructor(private readonly authRepo: AuthRepo) {}
  28 | 
  29 | 	/**
  30 | 	 * Validate invite code for user registration
  31 | 	 */
  32 | 	async validateInvite(inviteCode: string): Promise<InviteValidationResult> {
  33 | 		const invite = await this.authRepo.getInviteByCode(inviteCode);
  34 | 
  35 | 		if (!invite) {
  36 | 			return { valid: false, error: "Invalid invite code" };
  37 | 		}
  38 | 
  39 | 		if (invite.usedBy) {
  40 | 			return { valid: false, error: "Invite code has already been used" };
  41 | 		}
  42 | 
  43 | 		if (invite.expiresAt && new Date() > invite.expiresAt) {
  44 | 			return { valid: false, error: "Invite code has expired" };
  45 | 		}
  46 | 
  47 | 		return { valid: true, invite };
  48 | 	}
  49 | 
  50 | 	/**
  51 | 	 * Generate a unique invite code
  52 | 	 */
  53 | 	generateInviteCode(): string {
  54 | 		return crypto.randomUUID().slice(0, 8).toUpperCase();
  55 | 	}
  56 | 
  57 | 	/**
  58 | 	 * Calculate invite expiry date based on validity days
  59 | 	 */
  60 | 	calculateInviteExpiry(daysValid = 7): Date {
  61 | 		const expiry = new Date();
  62 | 		expiry.setDate(expiry.getDate() + daysValid);
  63 | 		return expiry;
  64 | 	}
  65 | 
  66 | 	/**
  67 | 	 * Validate user email format and uniqueness
  68 | 	 * (uniqueness check requires DB call through repo)
  69 | 	 */
  70 | 	async validateNewUser(email: string): Promise<{ valid: boolean; error?: string }> {
  71 | 		const emailRegex = /^[^\s@]+@([^\s@.,]+\.)+[^\s@.,]{2,}$/;
  72 | 		if (!emailRegex.test(email)) {
  73 | 			return { valid: false, error: "Invalid email format" };
  74 | 		}
  75 | 
  76 | 		const existingUser = await this.authRepo.getUserByEmail(email);
  77 | 		if (existingUser) {
  78 | 			return { valid: false, error: "User with this email already exists" };
  79 | 		}
  80 | 
  81 | 		return { valid: true };
  82 | 	}
  83 | 
  84 | 	/**
  85 | 	 * Check if admin onboarding is needed
  86 | 	 * Note: This requires access to config store - the repo should handle this
  87 | 	 */
  88 | 	async isAdminOnboardingRequired(): Promise<boolean> {
  89 | 		const adminCount = await this.authRepo.getAdminCount();
  90 | 		const isOnboarded = await adminRepo.getAdminOnboardedStatus();
  91 | 
  92 | 		return adminCount === 0 && !isOnboarded;
  93 | 	}
  94 | 
  95 | 	/**
  96 | 	 * Determine user role based on context
  97 | 	 */
  98 | 	determineUserRole(isFirstUser: boolean, requestedRole?: Role): Role {
  99 | 		if (isFirstUser) {
 100 | 			return "admin";
 101 | 		}
 102 | 		return requestedRole ?? "patient";
 103 | 	}
 104 | 
 105 | 	/**
 106 | 	 * Calculate quota limits based on user role
 107 | 	 */
 108 | 	getQuotaForRole(role: Role): { quota: number; fileCountQuota: number; inviteQuota: number } {
 109 | 		switch (role) {
 110 | 			case "admin":
 111 | 				return { quota: 10000, fileCountQuota: 10000, inviteQuota: 100 };
 112 | 			case "doctor":
 113 | 				return { quota: 5000, fileCountQuota: 5000, inviteQuota: 50 };
 114 | 			case "staff":
 115 | 				return { quota: 2000, fileCountQuota: 2000, inviteQuota: 20 };
 116 | 			default:
 117 | 				return { quota: 100, fileCountQuota: 100, inviteQuota: 0 };
 118 | 		}
 119 | 	}
 120 | 
 121 | 	/**
 122 | 	 * Validate clinic data
 123 | 	 */
 124 | 	validateClinicData(name: string, address: string, email?: string): { valid: boolean; error?: string } {
 125 | 		if (!name || name.trim().length < 2) {
 126 | 			return { valid: false, error: "Clinic name must be at least 2 characters" };
 127 | 		}
 128 | 		if (!address || address.trim().length < 5) {
 129 | 			return { valid: false, error: "Valid address is required" };
 130 | 		}
 131 | 		if (email) {
 132 | 			const emailRegex = /^[^\s@]+@([^\s@.,]+\.)+[^\s@.,]{2,}$/;
 133 | 			if (!emailRegex.test(email)) {
 134 | 				return { valid: false, error: "Invalid email format" };
 135 | 			}
 136 | 		}
 137 | 		return { valid: true };
 138 | 	}
 139 | 
 140 | 	/**
 141 | 	 * Generate a secure API key for a user
 142 | 	 */
 143 | 	generateApiKey(): string {
 144 | 		return `ak_${crypto.randomUUID().replace(/-/g, "")}`;
 145 | 	}
 146 | 
 147 | 	/**
 148 | 	 * Check if a user has permission to access a resource
 149 | 	 */
 150 | 	checkResourceAccess(
 151 | 		userRole: Role,
 152 | 		resourceOwnerId: string,
 153 | 		requestingUserId: string,
 154 | 		userClinicId?: string,
 155 | 		resourceClinicId?: string
 156 | 	): boolean {
 157 | 		// Admin has full access
 158 | 		if (userRole === "admin") return true;
 159 | 
 160 | 		// Users can access their own resources
 161 | 		if (requestingUserId === resourceOwnerId) return true;
 162 | 
 163 | 		// Clinic-scoped access for staff/doctors
 164 | 		if (userClinicId && resourceClinicId && userClinicId === resourceClinicId) {
 165 | 			return userRole === "doctor" || userRole === "staff";
 166 | 		}
 167 | 
 168 | 		return false;
 169 | 	}
 170 | 
 171 | 	/**
 172 | 	 * Sanitize user data for API responses (remove sensitive fields)
 173 | 	 */
 174 | 	sanitizeUser<T extends { apiKey?: string | null; twoFactorEnabled?: boolean | null }>(
 175 | 		user: T
 176 | 	): Omit<T, "apiKey"> & { hasTwoFactor: boolean } {
 177 | 		const { apiKey, twoFactorEnabled, ...rest } = user;
 178 | 		return {
 179 | 			...rest,
 180 | 			hasTwoFactor: twoFactorEnabled ?? false
 181 | 		} as Omit<T, "apiKey"> & { hasTwoFactor: boolean };
 182 | 	}
 183 | 
 184 | 	/**
 185 | 	 * Check if a session is expired
 186 | 	 */
 187 | 	isSessionExpired(expiresAt: Date): boolean {
 188 | 		return new Date() > expiresAt;
 189 | 	}
 190 | 
 191 | 	/**
 192 | 	 * Calculate session TTL based on remember me preference
 193 | 	 */
 194 | 	getSessionTTL(rememberMe = false): number {
 195 | 		// Return TTL in seconds
 196 | 		return rememberMe ? 30 * 24 * 60 * 60 : 7 * 24 * 60 * 60; // 30 days or 7 days
 197 | 	}
 198 | 	/**
 199 | 	 * Validate user email format
 200 | 	 */
 201 | 	validateEmail(email: string): boolean {
 202 | 		const emailRegex = /^[^\s@]+@([^\s@.,]+\.)+[^\s@.,]{2,}$/;
 203 | 		return emailRegex.test(email);
 204 | 	}
 205 | }
 206 | 
 207 | // Export singleton instance (will be properly injected later)
 208 | export const authService = new AuthService({} as AuthRepo);

```

`services/calendar.service.ts`:

```ts
   1 | // db/services/calendar.service.ts
   2 | // Pure business logic for calendar - no direct DB calls
   3 | 
   4 | import type { BookedSlot, TimeSlot } from "../schema";
   5 | 
   6 | /**
   7 |  * Service: Calendar Business Logic
   8 |  * - No direct DB calls
   9 |  * - Pure calculations and transformations
  10 |  */
  11 | export class CalendarService {
  12 | 	/**
  13 | 	 * Get event color based on event type
  14 | 	 */
  15 | 	getEventColor(type: string): string {
  16 | 		const colors: Record<string, string> = {
  17 | 			"well-child": "#10b981", // green
  18 | 			"sick-visit": "#3b82f6", // blue
  19 | 			"follow-up": "#8b5cf6", // purple
  20 | 			immunization: "#f59e0b", // orange
  21 | 			consultation: "#06b6d4" // cyan
  22 | 		};
  23 | 		return colors[type] || "#6b7280";
  24 | 	}
  25 | 
  26 | 	/**
  27 | 	 * Get status color based on appointment status
  28 | 	 */
  29 | 	getStatusColor(status: string): string {
  30 | 		const colors: Record<string, string> = {
  31 | 			SCHEDULED: "#3b82f6",
  32 | 			CONFIRMED: "#10b981",
  33 | 			COMPLETED: "#6b7280",
  34 | 			CANCELLED: "#ef4444",
  35 | 			NO_SHOW: "#ef4444"
  36 | 		};
  37 | 		return colors[status] || "#6b7280";
  38 | 	}
  39 | 
  40 | 	/**
  41 | 	 * Format event type for display
  42 | 	 */
  43 | 	formatEventType(type: string): string {
  44 | 		const formats: Record<string, string> = {
  45 | 			"well-child": "Well-Child Visit",
  46 | 			"sick-visit": "Sick Visit",
  47 | 			"follow-up": "Follow-up",
  48 | 			immunization: "Immunization",
  49 | 			consultation: "Consultation"
  50 | 		};
  51 | 		return formats[type] || type;
  52 | 	}
  53 | 
  54 | 	/**
  55 | 	 * Map event type to appointment type (database enum)
  56 | 	 */
  57 | 	mapEventTypeToAppointmentType(type: string): string {
  58 | 		const mapping: Record<string, string> = {
  59 | 			"well-child": "WELLNESS",
  60 | 			"sick-visit": "SICK",
  61 | 			"follow-up": "FOLLOW_UP",
  62 | 			immunization: "IMMUNIZATION",
  63 | 			consultation: "CONSULTATION"
  64 | 		};
  65 | 		return mapping[type] || "GENERAL";
  66 | 	}
  67 | 
  68 | 	/**
  69 | 	 * Map appointment type to event type (UI display)
  70 | 	 */
  71 | 	mapAppointmentTypeToEventType(type: string | null): string {
  72 | 		const mapping: Record<string, string> = {
  73 | 			WELLNESS: "well-child",
  74 | 			SICK: "sick-visit",
  75 | 			FOLLOW_UP: "follow-up",
  76 | 			IMMUNIZATION: "immunization",
  77 | 			CONSULTATION: "consultation"
  78 | 		};
  79 | 		return mapping[type || "GENERAL"] || "sick-visit";
  80 | 	}
  81 | 
  82 | 	/**
  83 | 	 * Calculate available time slots based on working hours and booked slots
  84 | 	 */
  85 | 	calculateAvailableSlots(
  86 | 		workingStart: string,
  87 | 		workingEnd: string,
  88 | 		durationMinutes: number,
  89 | 		bookedSlots: BookedSlot[]
  90 | 	): TimeSlot[] {
  91 | 		const slots: TimeSlot[] = [];
  92 | 
  93 | 		// Parse working hours
  94 | 		const [startHour, startMinute] = workingStart.split(":").map(Number);
  95 | 		const [endHour, endMinute] = workingEnd.split(":").map(Number);
  96 | 
  97 | 		const workingStartTime = new Date();
  98 | 		workingStartTime.setHours(startHour ?? 0, startMinute ?? 0, 0, 0);
  99 | 
 100 | 		const workingEndTime = new Date();
 101 | 		workingEndTime.setHours(endHour ?? 0, endMinute ?? 0, 0, 0);
 102 | 
 103 | 		// Sort booked slots by start time
 104 | 		const sortedBooked = [...bookedSlots].sort((a, b) => a.start.getTime() - b.start.getTime());
 105 | 
 106 | 		let currentStart = new Date(workingStartTime);
 107 | 
 108 | 		for (const booked of sortedBooked) {
 109 | 			const bookedEnd = new Date(booked.start.getTime() + (booked.duration || 30) * 60000);
 110 | 
 111 | 			// Check slot before booked appointment
 112 | 			if (currentStart < booked.start) {
 113 | 				const slotEnd = new Date(currentStart.getTime() + durationMinutes * 60000);
 114 | 				if (slotEnd <= booked.start) {
 115 | 					slots.push({ start: new Date(currentStart), end: slotEnd });
 116 | 				}
 117 | 				currentStart = bookedEnd;
 118 | 			} else if (currentStart < bookedEnd) {
 119 | 				currentStart = bookedEnd;
 120 | 			}
 121 | 		}
 122 | 
 123 | 		// Check remaining time after last booked appointment
 124 | 		while (currentStart.getTime() + durationMinutes * 60000 <= workingEndTime.getTime()) {
 125 | 			const slotEnd = new Date(currentStart.getTime() + durationMinutes * 60000);
 126 | 			slots.push({ start: new Date(currentStart), end: slotEnd });
 127 | 			currentStart = slotEnd;
 128 | 		}
 129 | 
 130 | 		return slots;
 131 | 	}
 132 | 
 133 | 	/**
 134 | 	 * Generate time slots for a day (every N minutes)
 135 | 	 */
 136 | 	generateTimeSlots(startTime: string, endTime: string, slotDurationMinutes = 30): string[] {
 137 | 		const slots: string[] = [];
 138 | 		const [startHour, startMinute] = startTime.split(":").map(Number);
 139 | 		const [endHour, endMinute] = endTime.split(":").map(Number);
 140 | 
 141 | 		let currentHour = startHour ?? 0;
 142 | 		let currentMinute = startMinute ?? 0;
 143 | 
 144 | 		while (currentHour < (endHour ?? 0) || (currentHour === (endHour ?? 0) && currentMinute < (endMinute ?? 0))) {
 145 | 			const time = `${currentHour.toString().padStart(2, "0")}:${currentMinute.toString().padStart(2, "0")}`;
 146 | 			slots.push(time);
 147 | 
 148 | 			currentMinute += slotDurationMinutes;
 149 | 			if (currentMinute >= 60) {
 150 | 				currentHour += Math.floor(currentMinute / 60);
 151 | 				currentMinute = currentMinute % 60;
 152 | 			}
 153 | 		}
 154 | 
 155 | 		return slots;
 156 | 	}
 157 | 
 158 | 	/**
 159 | 	 * Check if a time slot is within working hours
 160 | 	 */
 161 | 	isWithinWorkingHours(time: string, workingStart: string, workingEnd: string): boolean {
 162 | 		const [hour, minute] = time.split(":").map(Number);
 163 | 		const [startHour, startMinute] = workingStart.split(":").map(Number);
 164 | 		const [endHour, endMinute] = workingEnd.split(":").map(Number);
 165 | 
 166 | 		const timeMinutes = (hour ?? 0) * 60 + (minute ?? 0);
 167 | 		const startMinutes = (startHour ?? 0) * 60 + (startMinute ?? 0);
 168 | 		const endMinutes = (endHour ?? 0) * 60 + (endMinute ?? 0);
 169 | 
 170 | 		return timeMinutes >= startMinutes && timeMinutes + 30 <= endMinutes;
 171 | 	}
 172 | 
 173 | 	/**
 174 | 	 * Format date for calendar display
 175 | 	 */
 176 | 	formatCalendarDate(date: Date): string {
 177 | 		return date.toISOString().split("T")[0] ?? "";
 178 | 	}
 179 | 
 180 | 	/**
 181 | 	 * Get week days for a given date range
 182 | 	 */
 183 | 	getWeekDays(startDate: Date): Date[] {
 184 | 		const weekDays: Date[] = [];
 185 | 		const currentDate = new Date(startDate);
 186 | 
 187 | 		for (let i = 0; i < 7; i++) {
 188 | 			weekDays.push(new Date(currentDate));
 189 | 			currentDate.setDate(currentDate.getDate() + 1);
 190 | 		}
 191 | 
 192 | 		return weekDays;
 193 | 	}
 194 | 
 195 | 	/**
 196 | 	 * Calculate reminder times based on appointment date
 197 | 	 */
 198 | 	calculateReminderTimes(appointmentDate: Date, reminderHours: number[] = [24, 2]): Date[] {
 199 | 		return reminderHours.map(hours => {
 200 | 			const reminderTime = new Date(appointmentDate);
 201 | 			reminderTime.setHours(reminderTime.getHours() - hours);
 202 | 			return reminderTime;
 203 | 		});
 204 | 	}
 205 | 
 206 | 	/**
 207 | 	 * Get calendar event from appointment
 208 | 	 */
 209 | 	appointmentToCalendarEvent(appointment: {
 210 | 		id: string;
 211 | 		appointmentDate: Date;
 212 | 		time?: string | null;
 213 | 		type: string;
 214 | 		status: string;
 215 | 		durationMinutes?: number | null;
 216 | 		patient?: { firstName: string; lastName: string } | null;
 217 | 		doctor?: { name: string } | null;
 218 | 	}) {
 219 | 		const eventType = this.mapAppointmentTypeToEventType(appointment.type);
 220 | 		const startTime = appointment.time || "09:00";
 221 | 		const [hours, minutes] = startTime.split(":").map(Number);
 222 | 
 223 | 		const start = new Date(appointment.appointmentDate);
 224 | 		start.setHours(hours ?? 0, minutes ?? 0, 0, 0);
 225 | 
 226 | 		const end = new Date(start);
 227 | 		end.setMinutes(end.getMinutes() + (appointment.durationMinutes ?? 30));
 228 | 
 229 | 		return {
 230 | 			id: appointment.id,
 231 | 			title: `${eventType} ${appointment.patient ? `- ${appointment.patient.firstName} ${appointment.patient.lastName}` : ""}`,
 232 | 			start,
 233 | 			end,
 234 | 			type: eventType,
 235 | 			status: appointment.status,
 236 | 			color: this.getEventColor(eventType),
 237 | 			backgroundColor: this.getStatusColor(appointment.status),
 238 | 			extendedProps: {
 239 | 				appointmentType: appointment.type,
 240 | 				status: appointment.status,
 241 | 				patientName: appointment.patient
 242 | 					? `${appointment.patient.firstName} ${appointment.patient.lastName}`
 243 | 					: null,
 244 | 				doctorName: appointment.doctor?.name
 245 | 			}
 246 | 		};
 247 | 	}
 248 | 
 249 | 	/**
 250 | 	 * Get available time slots as formatted strings
 251 | 	 */
 252 | 	getAvailableTimeStrings(
 253 | 		workingStart: string,
 254 | 		workingEnd: string,
 255 | 		durationMinutes: number,
 256 | 		bookedSlots: BookedSlot[]
 257 | 	): string[] {
 258 | 		const availableSlots = this.calculateAvailableSlots(workingStart, workingEnd, durationMinutes, bookedSlots);
 259 | 
 260 | 		return availableSlots.map(slot => {
 261 | 			const hours = slot.start.getHours().toString().padStart(2, "0");
 262 | 			const minutes = slot.start.getMinutes().toString().padStart(2, "0");
 263 | 			return `${hours}:${minutes}`;
 264 | 		});
 265 | 	}
 266 | }
 267 | 
 268 | // Export singleton instance
 269 | export const calendarService = new CalendarService();

```

`services/config.service.ts`:

```ts
   1 | // db/services/config.service.ts
   2 | // Pure business logic for config management - no direct DB calls
   3 | 
   4 | import z from "zod";
   5 | 
   6 | import type { SystemRepo } from "../queries";
   7 | export type SnakeToCamelCase<S extends string> = S extends `${infer Head}_${infer Tail}`
   8 | 	? `${Lowercase<Head>}${Capitalize<SnakeToCamelCase<Tail>>}`
   9 | 	: Lowercase<S>;
  10 | 
  11 | export type CamelCaseKeys<T> =
  12 | 	T extends Array<infer U>
  13 | 		? CamelCaseKeys<U>[]
  14 | 		: T extends object
  15 | 			? {
  16 | 					[K in keyof T as SnakeToCamelCase<K & string>]: CamelCaseKeys<T[K]>;
  17 | 				}
  18 | 			: T;
  19 | 
  20 | export interface AppSettings {
  21 | 	requireInvite: boolean;
  22 | 	signUpEnabled: boolean;
  23 | 	blacklistedExtensions: string;
  24 | 	maxInviteAge: number;
  25 | 	allowUserCreateInvites: boolean;
  26 | 	defaultUserFileCountQuota: number;
  27 | 	defaultUserQuota: number;
  28 | 	defaultInvitesQuota: number;
  29 | 	uploadFileChunkSize: number;
  30 | 	uploadFileMaxSize: number;
  31 | 	cdnUrl: string;
  32 | 	baseUrl: string;
  33 | 	appName: string;
  34 | }
  35 | 
  36 | export interface ConfigDefinition {
  37 | 	key: string;
  38 | 	schema: z.ZodTypeAny;
  39 | 	defaultValue: unknown;
  40 | }
  41 | 
  42 | // Config definition as a constant
  43 | const CONFIG_DEF = {
  44 | 	require_invite: z.boolean().default(false),
  45 | 	sign_up_enabled: z.boolean().default(true),
  46 | 	blacklisted_extensions: z.string().default(""),
  47 | 	max_invite_age: z.number().default(1000 * 60 * 60 * 24 * 7),
  48 | 	allow_user_create_invites: z.boolean().default(true),
  49 | 	default_user_file_count_quota: z.number().default(1000),
  50 | 	default_user_quota: z.number().default(1024 * 1024 * 1024),
  51 | 	default_invites_quota: z.number().default(10),
  52 | 	upload_file_chunk_size: z.number().default(1024 * 1024 * 25),
  53 | 	upload_file_max_size: z.number().default(1024 * 1024 * 1024 * 5),
  54 | 	cdn_url: z.string().url().or(z.literal("")).default("")
  55 | };
  56 | 
  57 | type ConfigDef = typeof CONFIG_DEF;
  58 | 
  59 | /**
  60 |  * Service: Configuration Business Logic
  61 |  * - No direct DB calls
  62 |  * - Pure transformations and validations
  63 |  */
  64 | export class ConfigService {
  65 | 	private configDef: ConfigDef;
  66 | 	private settingsCache: AppSettings | null = null;
  67 | 	private cacheExpiry = 0;
  68 | 	private readonly CACHE_TTL = 60 * 1000; // 1 minute
  69 | 
  70 | 	constructor(
  71 | 		private readonly configRepo: SystemRepo,
  72 | 		configDef: ConfigDef = CONFIG_DEF
  73 | 	) {
  74 | 		this.configDef = configDef;
  75 | 	}
  76 | 
  77 | 	async getSettings(): Promise<AppSettings> {
  78 | 		// Check cache
  79 | 		if (this.settingsCache && Date.now() < this.cacheExpiry) {
  80 | 			return this.settingsCache;
  81 | 		}
  82 | 
  83 | 		// Fetch from database
  84 | 		const rows = await this.configRepo.getAllConfigs();
  85 | 		const configValues = this.rowsToConfig(rows);
  86 | 
  87 | 		// Get all keys that should exist
  88 | 		const allKeys = this.getConfigKeys();
  89 | 		const existingKeys = rows.map((r: { key: string }) => r.key);
  90 | 		const missingConfigs = this.getMissingKeys(existingKeys, allKeys);
  91 | 
  92 | 		if (missingConfigs.length > 0) {
  93 | 			await this.configRepo.initializeMissingConfigs(missingConfigs);
  94 | 		}
  95 | 
  96 | 		const settings = this.getAppSettingsWithEnv(configValues);
  97 | 
  98 | 		// Update cache
  99 | 		this.settingsCache = settings;
 100 | 		this.cacheExpiry = Date.now() + this.CACHE_TTL;
 101 | 
 102 | 		return settings;
 103 | 	}
 104 | 
 105 | 	/**
 106 | 	 * Invalidate cache (call after settings update)
 107 | 	 */
 108 | 	invalidateCache(): void {
 109 | 		this.settingsCache = null;
 110 | 		this.cacheExpiry = 0;
 111 | 	}
 112 | 
 113 | 	/**
 114 | 	 * Get a specific setting by key
 115 | 	 */
 116 | 	async getSetting<K extends keyof AppSettings>(key: K): Promise<AppSettings[K]> {
 117 | 		const settings = await this.getSettings();
 118 | 		return settings[key];
 119 | 	}
 120 | 
 121 | 	/**
 122 | 	 * Check if sign-up is enabled
 123 | 	 */
 124 | 	async isSignUpEnabled(): Promise<boolean> {
 125 | 		return this.getSetting("signUpEnabled");
 126 | 	}
 127 | 
 128 | 	/**
 129 | 	 * Check if invite is required
 130 | 	 */
 131 | 	async isInviteRequired(): Promise<boolean> {
 132 | 		return this.getSetting("requireInvite");
 133 | 	}
 134 | 
 135 | 	/**
 136 | 	 * Get max invite age
 137 | 	 */
 138 | 	async getMaxInviteAge(): Promise<number> {
 139 | 		return this.getSetting("maxInviteAge");
 140 | 	}
 141 | 
 142 | 	/**
 143 | 	 * Get default user quota
 144 | 	 */
 145 | 	async getDefaultUserQuota(): Promise<number> {
 146 | 		return this.getSetting("defaultUserQuota");
 147 | 	}
 148 | 
 149 | 	/**
 150 | 	 * Get default file count quota
 151 | 	 */
 152 | 	async getDefaultFileCountQuota(): Promise<number> {
 153 | 		return this.getSetting("defaultUserFileCountQuota");
 154 | 	}
 155 | 
 156 | 	/**
 157 | 	 * Get default invites quota
 158 | 	 */
 159 | 	async getDefaultInvitesQuota(): Promise<number> {
 160 | 		return this.getSetting("defaultInvitesQuota");
 161 | 	}
 162 | 
 163 | 	/**
 164 | 	 * Check if users can create invites
 165 | 	 */
 166 | 	async canUserCreateInvites(): Promise<boolean> {
 167 | 		return this.getSetting("allowUserCreateInvites");
 168 | 	}
 169 | 
 170 | 	/**
 171 | 	 * Convert snake_case to camelCase
 172 | 	 */
 173 | 	snakeToCamel(str: string): string {
 174 | 		return str.replace(/_([a-z])/g, g => g[1]?.toUpperCase() ?? "");
 175 | 	}
 176 | 
 177 | 	/**
 178 | 	 * Convert camelCase to snake_case
 179 | 	 */
 180 | 	camelToSnake(str: string): string {
 181 | 		return str.replace(/[A-Z]/g, letter => `_${letter.toLowerCase()}`);
 182 | 	}
 183 | 
 184 | 	/**
 185 | 	 * Parse a value according to its schema
 186 | 	 */
 187 | 	parseValue(value: string | undefined, schema: z.ZodTypeAny): unknown {
 188 | 		if (value === undefined || value === null) {
 189 | 			return schema.parse(undefined);
 190 | 		}
 191 | 
 192 | 		// Handle ZodDefault schemas
 193 | 		if (schema instanceof z.ZodDefault) {
 194 | 			const innerType = (schema as z.ZodDefault<z.ZodTypeAny>).def.innerType;
 195 | 			if (innerType instanceof z.ZodNumber) {
 196 | 				return Number(value);
 197 | 			}
 198 | 			if (innerType instanceof z.ZodBoolean) {
 199 | 				return value === "true";
 200 | 			}
 201 | 			if (innerType instanceof z.ZodString) {
 202 | 				return value;
 203 | 			}
 204 | 			return value;
 205 | 		}
 206 | 
 207 | 		// Handle direct types
 208 | 		if (schema instanceof z.ZodNumber) {
 209 | 			return Number(value);
 210 | 		}
 211 | 		if (schema instanceof z.ZodBoolean) {
 212 | 			return value === "true";
 213 | 		}
 214 | 		if (schema instanceof z.ZodString) {
 215 | 			return value;
 216 | 		}
 217 | 
 218 | 		return value;
 219 | 	}
 220 | 
 221 | 	/**
 222 | 	 * Get default values for all config keys
 223 | 	 */
 224 | 	getDefaults(): Record<string, unknown> {
 225 | 		const defaults: Record<string, unknown> = {};
 226 | 		for (const [key, schema] of Object.entries(this.configDef)) {
 227 | 			defaults[this.snakeToCamel(key)] = schema.parse(undefined);
 228 | 		}
 229 | 		return defaults;
 230 | 	}
 231 | 
 232 | 	/**
 233 | 	 * Get full AppSettings with defaults and environment variables
 234 | 	 */
 235 | 	getAppSettingsWithEnv(configValues: Record<string, unknown>): AppSettings {
 236 | 		const defaults = this.getDefaults();
 237 | 
 238 | 		return {
 239 | 			...defaults,
 240 | 			...configValues,
 241 | 			baseUrl: process.env.AUTH_BASE_URL ?? "",
 242 | 			appName: process.env.APP_NAME ?? ""
 243 | 		} as AppSettings;
 244 | 	}
 245 | 
 246 | 	/**
 247 | 	 * Transform database rows to config object
 248 | 	 */
 249 | 	rowsToConfig(rows: Array<{ key: string; value: string }>): Record<string, unknown> {
 250 | 		const finalConfig: Record<string, unknown> = {};
 251 | 		const rawMap = new Map(rows.map(r => [r.key, r.value]));
 252 | 
 253 | 		for (const [snakeKey, schema] of Object.entries(this.configDef)) {
 254 | 			const camelKey = this.snakeToCamel(snakeKey);
 255 | 			const dbValue = rawMap.get(snakeKey);
 256 | 			finalConfig[camelKey] = this.parseValue(dbValue, schema);
 257 | 		}
 258 | 
 259 | 		return finalConfig;
 260 | 	}
 261 | 
 262 | 	/**
 263 | 	 * Get missing config keys that need to be initialized
 264 | 	 */
 265 | 	getMissingKeys(existingKeys: string[], allKeys: string[]): Array<{ key: string; value: string }> {
 266 | 		const missing = allKeys.filter(k => !existingKeys.includes(k)) as (keyof ConfigDef)[];
 267 | 
 268 | 		return missing.map(key => ({
 269 | 			key,
 270 | 			value: String(this.parseValue(undefined, this.configDef[key]))
 271 | 		}));
 272 | 	}
 273 | 
 274 | 	/**
 275 | 	 * Validate config value against its schema
 276 | 	 */
 277 | 	validateConfigValue(key: string, value: unknown): { valid: boolean; error?: string } {
 278 | 		const schema = this.configDef[key as keyof ConfigDef];
 279 | 		if (!schema) {
 280 | 			return { valid: false, error: `Unknown config key: ${key}` };
 281 | 		}
 282 | 
 283 | 		try {
 284 | 			schema.parse(value);
 285 | 			return { valid: true };
 286 | 		} catch (error) {
 287 | 			return {
 288 | 				valid: false,
 289 | 				error: error instanceof Error ? error.message : "Invalid value"
 290 | 			};
 291 | 		}
 292 | 	}
 293 | 
 294 | 	/**
 295 | 	 * Serialize a value for database storage
 296 | 	 */
 297 | 	serializeValue(key: string, value: unknown): string {
 298 | 		const schema = this.configDef[key as keyof ConfigDef];
 299 | 		if (!schema) {
 300 | 			return String(value);
 301 | 		}
 302 | 
 303 | 		// Parse first to ensure it's valid
 304 | 		const parsed = schema.parse(value);
 305 | 		return String(parsed);
 306 | 	}
 307 | 
 308 | 	/**
 309 | 	 * Get config value by key with proper type
 310 | 	 */
 311 | 	getTypedValue<T>(key: string, configValues: Record<string, unknown>): T | undefined {
 312 | 		return configValues[this.snakeToCamel(key)] as T | undefined;
 313 | 	}
 314 | 
 315 | 	/**
 316 | 	 * Check if a config exists
 317 | 	 */
 318 | 	hasConfig(key: string, configValues: Record<string, unknown>): boolean {
 319 | 		return this.snakeToCamel(key) in configValues;
 320 | 	}
 321 | 
 322 | 	/**
 323 | 	 * Get all config keys
 324 | 	 */
 325 | 	getConfigKeys(): string[] {
 326 | 		return Object.keys(this.configDef);
 327 | 	}
 328 | 
 329 | 	/**
 330 | 	 * Get config definition for a key
 331 | 	 */
 332 | 	getConfigDefinition(key: string): ConfigDefinition | null {
 333 | 		const schema = this.configDef[key as keyof ConfigDef];
 334 | 		if (!schema) return null;
 335 | 
 336 | 		return {
 337 | 			key,
 338 | 			schema,
 339 | 			defaultValue: schema.parse(undefined)
 340 | 		};
 341 | 	}
 342 | 
 343 | 	/**
 344 | 	 * Convert config object to database rows
 345 | 	 */
 346 | 	configToRows(config: Record<string, unknown>): Array<{ key: string; value: string }> {
 347 | 		const rows: Array<{ key: string; value: string }> = [];
 348 | 
 349 | 		for (const [camelKey, value] of Object.entries(config)) {
 350 | 			// Find matching snake_case key
 351 | 			const snakeKey = Object.keys(this.configDef).find(k => this.snakeToCamel(k) === camelKey);
 352 | 			if (snakeKey) {
 353 | 				rows.push({
 354 | 					key: snakeKey,
 355 | 					value: this.serializeValue(snakeKey, value)
 356 | 				});
 357 | 			}
 358 | 		}
 359 | 
 360 | 		return rows;
 361 | 	}
 362 | 
 363 | 	/**
 364 | 	 * Merge user config with defaults
 365 | 	 */
 366 | 	mergeWithDefaults(userConfig: Partial<AppSettings>): AppSettings {
 367 | 		const defaults = this.getAppSettingsWithEnv({});
 368 | 		return {
 369 | 			...defaults,
 370 | 			...userConfig
 371 | 		} as AppSettings;
 372 | 	}
 373 | 
 374 | 	/**
 375 | 	 * Validate blacklisted extensions string
 376 | 	 */
 377 | 	validateBlacklistedExtensions(extensions: string): string[] {
 378 | 		if (!extensions) return [];
 379 | 		return extensions
 380 | 			.split(",")
 381 | 			.map(ext => ext.trim().toLowerCase())
 382 | 			.filter(ext => ext.length > 0);
 383 | 	}
 384 | 
 385 | 	/**
 386 | 	 * Check if an extension is blacklisted
 387 | 	 */
 388 | 	isExtensionBlacklisted(filename: string, blacklistedExtensions: string): boolean {
 389 | 		const extension = filename.split(".").pop()?.toLowerCase() ?? "";
 390 | 		const blacklisted = this.validateBlacklistedExtensions(blacklistedExtensions);
 391 | 		return blacklisted.includes(extension);
 392 | 	}
 393 | 
 394 | 	/**
 395 | 	 * Validate file size against config limits
 396 | 	 */
 397 | 	validateFileSize(size: number, maxSize: number, chunkSize: number): { valid: boolean; error?: string } {
 398 | 		if (size > maxSize) {
 399 | 			return {
 400 | 				valid: false,
 401 | 				error: `File size exceeds maximum allowed (${maxSize} bytes)`
 402 | 			};
 403 | 		}
 404 | 		if (size > chunkSize && chunkSize > 0) {
 405 | 			return {
 406 | 				valid: false,
 407 | 				error: `File must be uploaded in chunks of max ${chunkSize} bytes`
 408 | 			};
 409 | 		}
 410 | 		return { valid: true };
 411 | 	}
 412 | }
 413 | 
 414 | // Export singleton instance
 415 | export const configService = new ConfigService({} as SystemRepo);

```

`services/doctor.service.ts`:

```ts
   1 | // db/services/doctor.service.ts
   2 | // Pure business logic for doctor operations - no direct DB calls
   3 | 
   4 | import type { DoctorRepo } from "../queries/doctor.repo";
   5 | import type { AvailabilityStatus, Day } from "../schema";
   6 | 
   7 | export interface DoctorPerformance {
   8 | 	totalAppointments: number;
   9 | 	completedAppointments: number;
  10 | 	cancelledAppointments: number;
  11 | 	completionRate: number;
  12 | 	totalPrescriptions: number;
  13 | 	averageRating: number;
  14 | 	totalRevenue: number;
  15 | }
  16 | 
  17 | export interface WorkingHours {
  18 | 	day: Day;
  19 | 	startTime: string;
  20 | 	endTime: string;
  21 | }
  22 | 
  23 | export interface AvailabilitySlot {
  24 | 	startTime: string;
  25 | 	endTime: string;
  26 | 	available: boolean;
  27 | }
  28 | 
  29 | export interface DailySchedule {
  30 | 	date: Date;
  31 | 	workingHours: WorkingHours | null;
  32 | 	availableSlots: AvailabilitySlot[];
  33 | 	bookedSlots: Array<{ start: Date; duration: number }>;
  34 | }
  35 | 
  36 | /**
  37 |  * Service: Doctor Business Logic
  38 |  * - No direct DB calls
  39 |  * - Pure validations and calculations
  40 |  */
  41 | export class DoctorService {
  42 | 	constructor(private readonly doctorRepo: DoctorRepo) {}
  43 | 
  44 | 	/**
  45 | 	 * Validate doctor working hours
  46 | 	 */
  47 | 	validateWorkingHours(startTime: string, endTime: string): { valid: boolean; error?: string } {
  48 | 		const timeRegex = /^([0-1]?[0-9]|2[0-3]):[0-5][0-9]$/;
  49 | 
  50 | 		if (!timeRegex.test(startTime)) {
  51 | 			return { valid: false, error: "Invalid start time format" };
  52 | 		}
  53 | 
  54 | 		if (!timeRegex.test(endTime)) {
  55 | 			return { valid: false, error: "Invalid end time format" };
  56 | 		}
  57 | 
  58 | 		if (startTime >= endTime) {
  59 | 			return { valid: false, error: "Start time must be before end time" };
  60 | 		}
  61 | 
  62 | 		return { valid: true };
  63 | 	}
  64 | 
  65 | 	/**
  66 | 	 * Calculate doctor's availability status based on schedule and current time
  67 | 	 */
  68 | 	calculateAvailabilityStatus(workingDays: WorkingHours[], currentDate: Date = new Date()): AvailabilityStatus {
  69 | 		const dayOfWeek = currentDate.toLocaleDateString("en-US", { weekday: "long" }).toUpperCase() as Day;
  70 | 
  71 | 		const todayWorkingHours = workingDays.find(wd => wd.day === dayOfWeek);
  72 | 
  73 | 		if (!todayWorkingHours) {
  74 | 			return "UNAVAILABLE";
  75 | 		}
  76 | 
  77 | 		const currentTime = `${currentDate.getHours().toString().padStart(2, "0")}:${currentDate.getMinutes().toString().padStart(2, "0")}`;
  78 | 
  79 | 		if (currentTime >= todayWorkingHours.startTime && currentTime <= todayWorkingHours.endTime) {
  80 | 			return "AVAILABLE";
  81 | 		}
  82 | 
  83 | 		return "UNAVAILABLE";
  84 | 	}
  85 | 
  86 | 	/**
  87 | 	 * Calculate doctor performance metrics with additional insights
  88 | 	 */
  89 | 	calculatePerformanceMetrics(performance: DoctorPerformance): {
  90 | 		base: DoctorPerformance;
  91 | 		rating: string;
  92 | 		efficiency: number;
  93 | 		revenuePerAppointment: number;
  94 | 		prescriptionRate: number;
  95 | 	} {
  96 | 		const rating = this.getRatingLabel(performance.averageRating);
  97 | 		const efficiency =
  98 | 			performance.totalAppointments > 0
  99 | 				? (performance.completedAppointments / performance.totalAppointments) * 100
 100 | 				: 0;
 101 | 		const revenuePerAppointment =
 102 | 			performance.completedAppointments > 0 ? performance.totalRevenue / performance.completedAppointments : 0;
 103 | 		const prescriptionRate =
 104 | 			performance.completedAppointments > 0
 105 | 				? (performance.totalPrescriptions / performance.completedAppointments) * 100
 106 | 				: 0;
 107 | 
 108 | 		return {
 109 | 			base: performance,
 110 | 			rating,
 111 | 			efficiency: Math.round(efficiency * 10) / 10,
 112 | 			revenuePerAppointment: Math.round(revenuePerAppointment * 100) / 100,
 113 | 			prescriptionRate: Math.round(prescriptionRate * 10) / 10
 114 | 		};
 115 | 	}
 116 | 
 117 | 	/**
 118 | 	 * Get rating label from numeric rating
 119 | 	 */
 120 | 	getRatingLabel(rating: number): string {
 121 | 		if (rating >= 4.5) return "Excellent";
 122 | 		if (rating >= 4.0) return "Very Good";
 123 | 		if (rating >= 3.5) return "Good";
 124 | 		if (rating >= 3.0) return "Average";
 125 | 		if (rating >= 2.0) return "Below Average";
 126 | 		return "Poor";
 127 | 	}
 128 | 
 129 | 	/**
 130 | 	 * Generate available time slots for a day
 131 | 	 */
 132 | 	generateTimeSlots(
 133 | 		startTime: string,
 134 | 		endTime: string,
 135 | 		durationMinutes: number,
 136 | 		bookedSlots: Array<{ start: Date; duration: number }>,
 137 | 		date: Date
 138 | 	): AvailabilitySlot[] {
 139 | 		const slots: AvailabilitySlot[] = [];
 140 | 
 141 | 		const [startHour, startMinute] = startTime.split(":").map(Number);
 142 | 		const [endHour, endMinute] = endTime.split(":").map(Number);
 143 | 
 144 | 		let currentHour = startHour ?? 0;
 145 | 		let currentMinute = startMinute ?? 0;
 146 | 
 147 | 		const endTotalMinutes = (endHour ?? 0) * 60 + (endMinute ?? 0);
 148 | 
 149 | 		// Create a set of booked time ranges for quick lookup
 150 | 		const bookedRanges = bookedSlots.map(slot => ({
 151 | 			startMinutes: slot.start.getHours() * 60 + slot.start.getMinutes(),
 152 | 			endMinutes: slot.start.getHours() * 60 + slot.start.getMinutes() + slot.duration
 153 | 		}));
 154 | 
 155 | 		while (currentHour * 60 + currentMinute + durationMinutes <= endTotalMinutes) {
 156 | 			const slotStartMinutes = currentHour * 60 + currentMinute;
 157 | 			const slotEndMinutes = slotStartMinutes + durationMinutes;
 158 | 
 159 | 			// Check if slot overlaps with any booked slot
 160 | 			const isBooked = bookedRanges.some(
 161 | 				booked => !(slotEndMinutes <= booked.startMinutes || slotStartMinutes >= booked.endMinutes)
 162 | 			);
 163 | 
 164 | 			slots.push({
 165 | 				startTime: `${currentHour.toString().padStart(2, "0")}:${currentMinute.toString().padStart(2, "0")}`,
 166 | 				endTime: this.addMinutes(
 167 | 					`${currentHour.toString().padStart(2, "0")}:${currentMinute.toString().padStart(2, "0")}`,
 168 | 					durationMinutes
 169 | 				),
 170 | 				available: !isBooked
 171 | 			});
 172 | 
 173 | 			currentMinute += durationMinutes;
 174 | 			if (currentMinute >= 60) {
 175 | 				currentHour += Math.floor(currentMinute / 60);
 176 | 				currentMinute = currentMinute % 60;
 177 | 			}
 178 | 		}
 179 | 
 180 | 		return slots;
 181 | 	}
 182 | 
 183 | 	/**
 184 | 	 * Add minutes to a time string
 185 | 	 */
 186 | 	private addMinutes(time: string, minutes: number): string {
 187 | 		const [hours, mins] = time.split(":").map(Number);
 188 | 		const totalMinutes = (hours ?? 0) * 60 + (mins ?? 0) + minutes;
 189 | 		const newHours = Math.floor(totalMinutes / 60);
 190 | 		const newMins = totalMinutes % 60;
 191 | 		return `${newHours.toString().padStart(2, "0")}:${newMins.toString().padStart(2, "0")}`;
 192 | 	}
 193 | 
 194 | 	/**
 195 | 	 * Calculate working hours per week
 196 | 	 */
 197 | 	calculateWeeklyHours(workingDays: WorkingHours[]): {
 198 | 		totalHours: number;
 199 | 		daysWorked: number;
 200 | 		averageDailyHours: number;
 201 | 	} {
 202 | 		const daysWorked = workingDays.length;
 203 | 		const totalHours = workingDays.reduce((sum, day) => {
 204 | 			const [startHour, startMinute] = day.startTime.split(":").map(Number);
 205 | 			const [endHour, endMinute] = day.endTime.split(":").map(Number);
 206 | 			const hours = (endHour ?? 0) - (startHour ?? 0) + ((endMinute ?? 0) - (startMinute ?? 0)) / 60;
 207 | 			return sum + hours;
 208 | 		}, 0);
 209 | 
 210 | 		return {
 211 | 			totalHours: Math.round(totalHours * 10) / 10,
 212 | 			daysWorked,
 213 | 			averageDailyHours: daysWorked > 0 ? Math.round((totalHours / daysWorked) * 10) / 10 : 0
 214 | 		};
 215 | 	}
 216 | 
 217 | 	/**
 218 | 	 * Check if a doctor is available at a specific time
 219 | 	 */
 220 | 	isDoctorAvailableAtTime(
 221 | 		workingDays: WorkingHours[],
 222 | 		date: Date,
 223 | 		time: string
 224 | 	): { available: boolean; reason?: string } {
 225 | 		const dayOfWeek = date.toLocaleDateString("en-US", { weekday: "long" }).toUpperCase() as Day;
 226 | 
 227 | 		const todayHours = workingDays.find(wd => wd.day === dayOfWeek);
 228 | 
 229 | 		if (!todayHours) {
 230 | 			return { available: false, reason: "Doctor not scheduled on this day" };
 231 | 		}
 232 | 
 233 | 		if (time < todayHours.startTime || time > todayHours.endTime) {
 234 | 			return { available: false, reason: "Time is outside working hours" };
 235 | 		}
 236 | 
 237 | 		return { available: true };
 238 | 	}
 239 | 
 240 | 	/**
 241 | 	 * Format doctor specialty for display
 242 | 	 */
 243 | 	formatSpecialty(specialty: string): string {
 244 | 		const specialtyMap: Record<string, string> = {
 245 | 			Pediatrics: "Pediatrician",
 246 | 			Cardiology: "Cardiologist",
 247 | 			Dermatology: "Dermatologist",
 248 | 			Neurology: "Neurologist",
 249 | 			Orthopedics: "Orthopedic Surgeon",
 250 | 			Gynecology: "Gynecologist",
 251 | 			Psychiatry: "Psychiatrist",
 252 | 			"General Medicine": "General Physician",
 253 | 			Surgery: "Surgeon",
 254 | 			Oncology: "Oncologist",
 255 | 			Endocrinology: "Endocrinologist",
 256 | 			Gastroenterology: "Gastroenterologist",
 257 | 			Pulmonology: "Pulmonologist",
 258 | 			Rheumatology: "Rheumatologist",
 259 | 			Urology: "Urologist"
 260 | 		};
 261 | 
 262 | 		return specialtyMap[specialty] || specialty;
 263 | 	}
 264 | 
 265 | 	/**
 266 | 	 * Calculate doctor's workload based on appointments
 267 | 	 */
 268 | 	calculateWorkload(
 269 | 		dailyAppointments: number,
 270 | 		maxCapacity = 20
 271 | 	): {
 272 | 		loadPercentage: number;
 273 | 		status: "low" | "moderate" | "high" | "overloaded";
 274 | 		recommendation: string;
 275 | 	} {
 276 | 		const loadPercentage = (dailyAppointments / maxCapacity) * 100;
 277 | 
 278 | 		let status: "low" | "moderate" | "high" | "overloaded";
 279 | 		let recommendation: string;
 280 | 
 281 | 		if (loadPercentage <= 30) {
 282 | 			status = "low";
 283 | 			recommendation = "Consider marketing to increase patient volume";
 284 | 		} else if (loadPercentage <= 60) {
 285 | 			status = "moderate";
 286 | 			recommendation = "Maintain current schedule";
 287 | 		} else if (loadPercentage <= 85) {
 288 | 			status = "high";
 289 | 			recommendation = "Consider adding more available slots";
 290 | 		} else {
 291 | 			status = "overloaded";
 292 | 			recommendation = "Reduce appointment slots or add staff";
 293 | 		}
 294 | 
 295 | 		return {
 296 | 			loadPercentage: Math.round(loadPercentage * 10) / 10,
 297 | 			status,
 298 | 			recommendation
 299 | 		};
 300 | 	}
 301 | }
 302 | 
 303 | // Export singleton instance
 304 | export const doctorService = new DoctorService({} as DoctorRepo);
```

`services/labs.service.ts`:

```ts
   1 | // db/services/labs.service.ts
   2 | // Pure business logic for lab tests - no direct DB calls
   3 | 
   4 | export interface LabTestResult {
   5 | 	testId: string;
   6 | 	patientId: string;
   7 | 	testName: string;
   8 | 	result: string;
   9 | 	referenceRange?: string;
  10 | 	isAbnormal: boolean;
  11 | 	severity?: "normal" | "borderline" | "abnormal" | "critical";
  12 | }
  13 | 
  14 | export interface LabTrend {
  15 | 	testName: string;
  16 | 	values: Array<{ date: Date; value: number }>;
  17 | 	trend: "increasing" | "decreasing" | "stable";
  18 | 	percentageChange: number;
  19 | }
  20 | 
  21 | /**
  22 |  * Service: Lab Test Business Logic
  23 |  * - No direct DB calls
  24 |  * - Pure calculations and validations
  25 |  */
  26 | export class LabsService {
  27 | 	/**
  28 | 	 * Determine if a lab result is abnormal based on reference ranges
  29 | 	 */
  30 | 	analyzeLabResult(value: number, referenceRange: string, unit?: string): LabTestResult {
  31 | 		const parsed = this.parseReferenceRange(referenceRange);
  32 | 		const isAbnormal = parsed ? value < parsed.min || value > parsed.max : false;
  33 | 
  34 | 		let severity: "normal" | "borderline" | "abnormal" | "critical" = "normal";
  35 | 		if (isAbnormal) {
  36 | 			if (
  37 | 				parsed &&
  38 | 				((parsed.criticalMin !== undefined && value < parsed.criticalMin) ||
  39 | 					(parsed.criticalMax !== undefined && value > parsed.criticalMax))
  40 | 			) {
  41 | 				severity = "critical";
  42 | 			} else if (
  43 | 				parsed &&
  44 | 				((parsed.abnormalMin !== undefined && value < parsed.abnormalMin) ||
  45 | 					(parsed.abnormalMax !== undefined && value > parsed.abnormalMax))
  46 | 			) {
  47 | 				severity = "abnormal";
  48 | 			} else if (
  49 | 				parsed &&
  50 | 				((parsed.borderlineMin !== undefined && value < parsed.borderlineMin) ||
  51 | 					(parsed.borderlineMax !== undefined && value > parsed.borderlineMax))
  52 | 			) {
  53 | 				severity = "borderline";
  54 | 			} else {
  55 | 				severity = "abnormal";
  56 | 			}
  57 | 		}
  58 | 
  59 | 		return {
  60 | 			testId: "",
  61 | 			patientId: "",
  62 | 			testName: "",
  63 | 			result: `${value} ${unit || ""}`,
  64 | 			referenceRange,
  65 | 			isAbnormal,
  66 | 			severity
  67 | 		};
  68 | 	}
  69 | 
  70 | 	/**
  71 | 	 * Parse reference range string (e.g., "4.5-11.0" or "<5" or ">10")
  72 | 	 */
  73 | 	parseReferenceRange(range: string): {
  74 | 		min: number;
  75 | 		max: number;
  76 | 		borderlineMin?: number;
  77 | 		borderlineMax?: number;
  78 | 		abnormalMin?: number;
  79 | 		abnormalMax?: number;
  80 | 		criticalMin?: number;
  81 | 		criticalMax?: number;
  82 | 	} | null {
  83 | 		if (!range) return null;
  84 | 
  85 | 		const match = range.match(/([\d.]+)\s*-\s*([\d.]+)/);
  86 | 		if (match) {
  87 | 			return {
  88 | 				min: Number.parseFloat(match[1]!),
  89 | 				max: Number.parseFloat(match[2]!),
  90 | 				borderlineMin: Number.parseFloat(match[1]!) * 0.9,
  91 | 				borderlineMax: Number.parseFloat(match[2]!) * 1.1,
  92 | 				abnormalMin: Number.parseFloat(match[1]!) * 0.8,
  93 | 				abnormalMax: Number.parseFloat(match[2]!) * 1.2,
  94 | 				criticalMin: Number.parseFloat(match[1]!) * 0.5,
  95 | 				criticalMax: Number.parseFloat(match[2]!) * 1.5
  96 | 			};
  97 | 		}
  98 | 
  99 | 		const lessThan = range.match(/<([\d.]+)/);
 100 | 		if (lessThan) {
 101 | 			return { min: 0, max: Number.parseFloat(lessThan[1]!) };
 102 | 		}
 103 | 
 104 | 		const greaterThan = range.match(/>([\d.]+)/);
 105 | 		if (greaterThan) {
 106 | 			return { min: Number.parseFloat(greaterThan[1]!), max: Number.POSITIVE_INFINITY };
 107 | 		}
 108 | 
 109 | 		return null;
 110 | 	}
 111 | 
 112 | 	/**
 113 | 	 * Calculate test trends over time
 114 | 	 */
 115 | 	calculateTrend(historicalResults: Array<{ date: Date; value: number }>): LabTrend | null {
 116 | 		if (historicalResults.length < 2) return null;
 117 | 
 118 | 		const sorted = [...historicalResults].sort((a, b) => a.date.getTime() - b.date.getTime());
 119 | 		const first = sorted[0]!;
 120 | 		const last = sorted[sorted.length - 1]!;
 121 | 
 122 | 		const percentageChange = first.value > 0 ? ((last.value - first.value) / first.value) * 100 : 0;
 123 | 
 124 | 		let trend: "increasing" | "decreasing" | "stable" = "stable";
 125 | 		if (percentageChange > 10) trend = "increasing";
 126 | 		else if (percentageChange < -10) trend = "decreasing";
 127 | 
 128 | 		return {
 129 | 			testName: "",
 130 | 			values: sorted,
 131 | 			trend,
 132 | 			percentageChange
 133 | 		};
 134 | 	}
 135 | 
 136 | 	/**
 137 | 	 * Generate interpretation for lab result
 138 | 	 */
 139 | 	generateInterpretation(testName: string, value: number, referenceRange: string): string {
 140 | 		const parsed = this.parseReferenceRange(referenceRange);
 141 | 		if (!parsed) return "No reference range available for interpretation.";
 142 | 
 143 | 		if (value < parsed.min) {
 144 | 			return `Low ${testName} level. ${this.getLowLevelSuggestion(testName)}`;
 145 | 		}
 146 | 		if (value > parsed.max) {
 147 | 			return `Elevated ${testName} level. ${this.getHighLevelSuggestion(testName)}`;
 148 | 		}
 149 | 		return `${testName} is within normal reference range.`;
 150 | 	}
 151 | 
 152 | 	private getLowLevelSuggestion(testName: string): string {
 153 | 		const suggestions: Record<string, string> = {
 154 | 			hemoglobin: "May indicate anemia. Consider iron supplementation.",
 155 | 			glucose: "May indicate hypoglycemia. Monitor blood sugar levels.",
 156 | 			potassium: "May indicate hypokalemia. Review medications and diet.",
 157 | 			calcium: "May indicate hypocalcemia. Consider calcium supplementation.",
 158 | 			sodium: "May indicate hyponatremia. Review fluid intake."
 159 | 		};
 160 | 		return suggestions[testName.toLowerCase()] || "Further evaluation recommended.";
 161 | 	}
 162 | 
 163 | 	private getHighLevelSuggestion(testName: string): string {
 164 | 		const suggestions: Record<string, string> = {
 165 | 			hemoglobin: "May indicate polycythemia. Monitor and evaluate.",
 166 | 			glucose: "May indicate hyperglycemia. Evaluate for diabetes.",
 167 | 			potassium: "May indicate hyperkalemia. Review medications immediately.",
 168 | 			calcium: "May indicate hypercalcemia. Investigate underlying cause.",
 169 | 			sodium: "May indicate hypernatremia. Assess hydration status."
 170 | 		};
 171 | 		return suggestions[testName.toLowerCase()] || "Further evaluation recommended.";
 172 | 	}
 173 | 
 174 | 	/**
 175 | 	 * Determine if follow-up testing is needed
 176 | 	 */
 177 | 	needsFollowUp(result: LabTestResult): { needed: boolean; suggestedDays: number; reason?: string } {
 178 | 		if (result.severity === "critical") {
 179 | 			return { needed: true, suggestedDays: 1, reason: "Critical result requires immediate follow-up" };
 180 | 		}
 181 | 		if (result.severity === "abnormal") {
 182 | 			return { needed: true, suggestedDays: 7, reason: "Abnormal result requires follow-up within a week" };
 183 | 		}
 184 | 		if (result.severity === "borderline") {
 185 | 			return { needed: true, suggestedDays: 30, reason: "Borderline result - monitor and recheck in a month" };
 186 | 		}
 187 | 		return { needed: false, suggestedDays: 0 };
 188 | 	}
 189 | 
 190 | 	/**
 191 | 	 * Calculate turnaround time for lab test
 192 | 	 */
 193 | 	calculateTurnaroundTime(orderedAt: Date, resultedAt: Date): number {
 194 | 		const diffHours = (resultedAt.getTime() - orderedAt.getTime()) / (1000 * 60 * 60);
 195 | 		return Math.round(diffHours * 10) / 10;
 196 | 	}
 197 | 
 198 | 	/**
 199 | 	 * Get status based on turnaround time
 200 | 	 */
 201 | 	getTurnaroundStatus(hours: number, expectedHours = 24): "on-time" | "delayed" | "critical" {
 202 | 		if (hours <= expectedHours) return "on-time";
 203 | 		if (hours <= expectedHours * 2) return "delayed";
 204 | 		return "critical";
 205 | 	}
 206 | }
 207 | 
 208 | export const labsService = new LabsService();

```

`utils/age.ts`:

```ts
   1 | export function calculateDateRange(period: string, startDate?: Date, endDate?: Date): { start: Date; end: Date } {
   2 | 	const end = endDate || new Date();
   3 | 	const start =
   4 | 		startDate ||
   5 | 		(() => {
   6 | 			const d = new Date();
   7 | 			switch (period) {
   8 | 				case "week":
   9 | 					d.setDate(d.getDate() - 7);
  10 | 					break;
  11 | 				case "month":
  12 | 					d.setMonth(d.getMonth() - 1);
  13 | 					break;
  14 | 				case "quarter":
  15 | 					d.setMonth(d.getMonth() - 3);
  16 | 					break;
  17 | 				case "year":
  18 | 					d.setFullYear(d.getFullYear() - 1);
  19 | 					break;
  20 | 				case "6months":
  21 | 					d.setMonth(d.getMonth() - 6);
  22 | 					break;
  23 | 				default:
  24 | 					d.setMonth(d.getMonth() - 1);
  25 | 			}
  26 | 			return d;
  27 | 		})();
  28 | 	return { start, end };
  29 | }
  30 | 
  31 | export function groupDataByDate<T extends { date?: Date; appointmentDate?: Date; createdAt?: Date }>(
  32 | 	items: T[],
  33 | 	groupBy: string,
  34 | 	valueExtractor: (item: T) => number
  35 | ): Array<{ date: string; value: number; label: string }> {
  36 | 	const grouped = new Map<string, number>();
  37 | 
  38 | 	for (const item of items) {
  39 | 		const rawDate = item.date || item.appointmentDate || item.createdAt;
  40 | 		if (!rawDate) continue;
  41 | 
  42 | 		const date = new Date(rawDate);
  43 | 		let key: string;
  44 | 
  45 | 		if (groupBy === "day") {
  46 | 			key = date.toISOString().split("T")[0] || "";
  47 | 		} else if (groupBy === "week") {
  48 | 			const weekStart = new Date(date);
  49 | 			weekStart.setDate(date.getDate() - date.getDay());
  50 | 			key = weekStart.toISOString().split("T")[0] || "";
  51 | 		} else {
  52 | 			key = `${date.getFullYear()}-${date.getMonth() + 1}`;
  53 | 		}
  54 | 
  55 | 		grouped.set(key, (grouped.get(key) || 0) + valueExtractor(item));
  56 | 	}
  57 | 
  58 | 	return Array.from(grouped.entries())
  59 | 		.map(([key, value]) => ({ date: key, value, label: key }))
  60 | 		.sort((a, b) => a.date.localeCompare(b.date));
  61 | }
  62 | 
  63 | export function calculateTrend(current: number, previous: number): "improving" | "declining" | "stable" {
  64 | 	if (current > previous * 1.05) return "improving";
  65 | 	if (current < previous * 0.95) return "declining";
  66 | 	return "stable";
  67 | }
```

`utils/appointment.ts`:

```ts
   1 | import { db } from "../client";
   2 | 
   3 | export async function getTimeLeftForAppointment(appointmentId: string): Promise<string> {
   4 | 	const record = await db.query.appointment.findFirst({
   5 | 		with: {
   6 | 			patient: true,
   7 | 			doctor: true,
   8 | 			clinic: true,
   9 | 			service: true
  10 | 		},
  11 | 		where: { id: appointmentId }
  12 | 	});
  13 | 
  14 | 	if (!record?.appointmentDate) {
  15 | 		return "Appointment not found or date missing";
  16 | 	}
  17 | 
  18 | 	const now = new Date();
  19 | 	const appointmentTime = new Date(record.appointmentDate);
  20 | 
  21 | 	const diffMs = appointmentTime.getTime() - now.getTime();
  22 | 	if (diffMs < 0) return "Appointment has already passed";
  23 | 
  24 | 	const diffMins = Math.floor(diffMs / (1000 * 60));
  25 | 	const hours = Math.floor(diffMins / 60);
  26 | 	const mins = diffMins % 60;
  27 | 
  28 | 	if (hours > 0) {
  29 | 		return `${hours} hours and ${mins} minutes remaining`;
  30 | 	}
  31 | 	return `${mins} minutes remaining`;
  32 | }
```
